description: This figure presents EBSD characterization results of three Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) austenitic low-density steels (28Mn0Ni, 28Mn5Ni, 15Mn5Ni) subjected to hot rolling (HR), cold rolling (CR), and subsequent annealing at 973 K or 1173 K. Subfigures (a), (c), (e) are inverse pole figure (IPF) maps of austenite for 28Mn0Ni, 28Mn5Ni, 15Mn5Ni after HR+CR+973 K treatment, respectively, while (b), (d), (f) are corresponding austenite IPF maps after HR+CR+1173 K treatment, with an inset in (f) showing IPF map of B2 phase alone revealing its grain recovery. Subfigures (g), (h) are phase maps of 15Mn5Ni after 973 K and 1173 K annealing, respectively, where blue represents B2 phase and red represents FCC austenite, with a 20 μm scale bar for all micrographs. It is observed that as annealing temperature rises from 973 K to 1173 K, austenite recrystallization fraction and grain size increase, while B2 phase dissolves partially. B2 phase is more stable in lower-Mn higher-Ni 15Mn5Ni alloy, and B2-free 28Mn0Ni shows more significant austenite grain growth at 1173 K, confirming that B2 precipitates suppress austenite grain boundary migration, and two-phase region annealing enables grain refinement of low-density steels.
